name: Python/PyPI Supply Chain Attacks
description: Known malicious PyPI packages and supply chain compromises

malicious_packages.name: ctx
malicious_packages.notes: Typosquat/takeover of abandoned ctx package. Harvested AWS credentials from env vars. May 2022.

malicious_packages.name: phpass
malicious_packages.notes: Typosquat of PHP password hashing lib. Targeted Python devs familiar with PHP.

malicious_packages.name: request
malicious_packages.notes: PyPI typosquat of the popular npm 'request' package. ALL versions malicious. Exfiltrates env vars.

malicious_packages.name: requests-unverified
malicious_packages.notes: Typosquat of 'requests'. Disables SSL verification and exfiltrates data.

malicious_packages.name: importantpackage
malicious_packages.notes: Harvests system info and env vars on install

malicious_packages.name: pyjtorch
malicious_packages.notes: Typosquat of PyTorch. Credential and key harvesting.

malicious_packages.name: torch-directml
malicious_packages.notes: Suspicious package mimicking official PyTorch DirectML support

malicious_packages.name: loglib-modules
malicious_packages.notes: Targeted Discord users. Harvests Discord tokens, credit card data, browser passwords.

malicious_packages.name: discord-selfbot-v14
malicious_packages.notes: Harvests Discord tokens and account info

malicious_packages.name: yiffparty
malicious_packages.notes: Targets macOS. Exfiltrates browser cookies, discord tokens, clipboard.

malicious_packages.name: setup-tools
malicious_packages.notes: Typosquat of setuptools (missing 'up'). Executes reverse shell.

malicious_packages.name: noblesse
malicious_packages.notes: Steals credentials, Discord tokens, browser stored passwords, PayPal accounts

suspicious_packages.reason: Common pattern for malicious Discord library variants

network_iocs.context: Used by various PyPI stealers for IP logging

file_artifacts.context: Common output file pattern for Python stealers
